Standard Description: Solve problems involving irrational numbers and rational approximations.
Solve problems involving radicals and integer exponents including scientific notation.
Create and interpret expressions and equations to solve real-world problems.
Understand the concept of a function and use functions to describe quantitative relationships.
Explain congruency and similarity using physical models or software.
Apply the Pythagorean Theorem to determine unknown side lengths in right triangles.
Apply the formulas for the volume of cones cylinders and spheres to solve real-world problems.
Investigate patterns of association in bivariate data.
